content: Hi, I'm feeling a bit uncertain about my career as a Professional Rock Climber and Guide. I love the work, but I'm not sure if it's sustainable long-term.
I understand. Being a professional rock climber can be a challenging and unpredictable career path. What are some of the things that are making you question whether it's sustainable long-term?

I think it's just the physical demands of the job. I've been doing it for years and I'm starting to feel the wear and tear on my body. I've also been learning Spanish, which has been a great experience, but it's made me realize that I might want to explore other career options that don't involve as much physical risk.
That makes sense. It's great that you're taking steps to prioritize your physical health and well-being. Learning a new language is a big undertaking, how's that going?

It's going well, thanks for asking! I'm really enjoying it and I feel like it's opening up a lot of new opportunities for me. I've even been able to practice with some of my clients, like my friend, Alex, who is a fellow climber.
That's great to hear! It's always helpful to have a support system when learning a new language. What kind of career options are you considering that don't involve as much physical risk?
I've been thinking about teaching or coaching, maybe even starting my own climbing school. I've also been interested in urban sketching and I've thought about pursuing that as a career.
That's an interesting idea! Starting your own business can be a lot of work, but it can also be very rewarding. What kind of experience do you have with teaching or coaching?
I've been guiding and teaching climbing for years, so I have a lot of experience with that. I've also been doing some urban sketching on the side and I've really enjoyed it.
That's great to hear! It's always helpful to have a passion project that you can work on in your free time. What kind of goals do you have for your potential business?

I'd love to have it up and running by the time I move to Taiwan, maybe even have a small storefront in Taipei. I've been looking at a few different locations and I'm excited to see what the future holds.
That sounds like a great goal! Taipei is a beautiful city, I'm sure you'll love it there. What kind of challenges do you think you might face when starting your business in a new country?

I'm not sure, I've been trying to research as much as I can, but I'm sure there will be some unexpected challenges. I've been talking to some of my friends who have started businesses in Taiwan and they've given me some great advice.
